tool_args.label: Source: 181st Street Busway CAB Meeting June 2022

payload.title: NYC DOT 181st Street Busway Community Advisory Board Meeting
payload.description: Presentation deck for the 181st Street Busway Community Advisory Board meeting held June 23, 2022. Covers background, busway updates, on-street outreach, and next steps.
Bus priority corridor on 181st Street, launched April 26, 2021. Proposed to be made permanent with reduced hours of 6 AM – 8 PM, 7 days/week based on community feedback.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

tool_args.label: Council Member Carmen De La Rosa

payload.description: Bus stop consolidation on 181st St / St. Nicholas Ave. Included bench relocation and new Leaning Bar
Gantry signage installed approaching 181st St/Audubon Ave and at 181st St/Wadsworth Ave to increase visibility of busway regulations

tool_args.label: Proposed reduced Busway hours: 6 AM – 8 PM, 7 days/week

payload.description: Reduce Busway hours based on community feedback. New regulations: 6 AM – 8 PM, 7-days/week. Move to make Busway pilot permanent.

tool_args.label: Curb regulation adjustment at 181st St/Audubon Ave

payload.description: Adjust curb regulation on southwest corner of 181st St/Audubon Ave
Adjust commercial loading hours to afternoon and allow morning parking

tool_args.label: Work with ConEd on construction material storage

payload.description: Work with ConEd to explore alternatives to on-street storage of construction materials to free up parking spaces

tool_args.label: Continue to monitor traffic on side streets

payload.description: Continue to monitor traffic on side streets
